text: What is stored inside a Splunk Enterprise index bucket?

options.text: KV Store, index files and NoSQL database files
Bloom filter, tsidx and compressed raw data (and some other stuff)
Inverted indices and BKD trees
B-tree, data pages and SQL indices

text: A Bloom filter is:

options.text: A new cartoon filter on Instagram
A filter for ActiveX objects or Java applets
A probabilistic data structure based on hashing
Car oil filter for electric cars

text: What is the correct order of Splunk data lifecycle stages?

options.text: Hot → Cold → Warm → Frozen
Warm → Hot → Cold → Frozen
Hot → Warm → Cold → Frozen
Hot → Warm → Frozen → Cold

text: TERM() directive can be used across major breakers (whitespace, commas, etc.)

options.text: Yes
No

text: Streaming commands should be used BEFORE transforming commands in the pipeline

options.text: Yes
No

text: Which search is faster and why?

options.text: index=s4c_weather | stats avg(main.temp) by name
| tstats avg(main.temp) WHERE index=s4c_weather by name
They are exactly the same speed
The first — tstats only works on datamodels

text: tstats is best used at the END of a search pipeline, after streaming commands

options.text: Yes
No — tstats is a generating command and must be FIRST

text: What are the 'apocalyptic horsemen' of bad searches? (select all that apply)

options.text: index=* or missing index filter
All Time search range
Wildcards at the beginning or in the middle of a term
Using JOIN to combine different data sources

text: Which search mode is tuned for the fastest results and skips non-essential field processing?

options.text: Verbose mode
Smart mode — always the fastest choice
Night mode
Fast mode

text: Your best helper for diagnosing slow searches is called:

options.text: Monitoring Console
Job Inspector
Christoph
SPL Profiler

text: What is NOT true about Splunk Metrics?

options.text: Metrics are great for performance
Metrics work well in the Analytics Workspace
Metrics require additional software to use in Splunk Enterprise
Context information can be stored as a dimension

text: Dashboard Studio source code is written in:

options.text: XML
YAML
Estonian
JSON

text: A Chain Search in Dashboard Studio is:

options.text: A very slow search that triggers a chain reaction
The new name for a post-processing search in Dashboard Studio
A search across supply chain data
An AI-based search combining Google and Bing

text: In SmartStore, the cache manager mainly:

options.text: Fetches warm buckets from the remote store into local cache and evicts them by policy
Runs all user searches so search heads stay idle
Replaces the license manager
Only moves frozen archives to object storage

text: A generating command is special because it:

options.text: Runs only on the search head captain
Must always be followed immediately by stats
Cannot run in distributed search environments
Starts a new result set and is often required as the first command

text: Dashboard Studio images uploaded only through the UI (stored in KV Store) are a problem when you move the dashboard because:

options.text: They auto-sync to your Git repository and cause conflicts
They may not come along when you package or install the app elsewhere
They always live as files under etc/apps
They delete the dashboard JSON for security reasons

text: In Splunk Metrics, dimensions are:

options.text: Numeric measurement values at a timestamp
Dot-separated metric name segments
Key-value pairs that add contextual metadata to measurements
Index configuration parameters

text: Which of the following are valid Splunk search tips? (select all that apply)

options.text: Always specify the index you need
Restrict the time range as much as possible
Use wildcards as prefixes for flexible matching
Place streaming commands earlier in the pipeline

text: PREFIX() in tstats allows you to:

options.text: Add a custom prefix to every event field name
Treat indexed terms as key-value pairs without requiring an indexed field
Filter searches to a specific index prefix
Pre-sort events before aggregation

text: The Splunk data pipeline processes data in which order?

options.text: Index Pipeline → Parsing Pipeline → Index Queue → Parsing Queue
Parsing Queue → Index Queue → Parsing Pipeline → Index Pipeline
Parsing Queue → Parsing Pipeline → Index Queue → Index Pipeline
Index Queue → Parsing Queue → Index Pipeline → Parsing Pipeline
